---
title: "Cell type annotation"
author: "Cankun Wang"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%m/%d/%Y')`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(Seurat)
library(cowplot)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(here)
library(qs)
library(Polychrome)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tidyverse)
```


# Set working dir

```{r,echo=F,eval=T,message=F,warning=F,error=F}

here::set_here()
print(paste("Current working directory:", here::here()))

```

# Load data

```{r,echo=F,eval=T,message=F,warning=F,error=F}
source("functions.R")
####

#combined <- readRDS("combined.rds")
combined <- qs::qread('combined.qsave')
####

DefaultAssay(combined) <- "RNA"
custom_color <-
  as.character(palette36.colors(36)[-2])[1:length(levels(Idents(combined)))]
Idents(combined) <- combined$seurat_clusters
```

# Identify cell clusters

```{r,echo=F,eval=F,message=FALSE,warning=F}

dbs <-
  c(
    "GO_Molecular_Function_2018",
    "GO_Cellular_Component_2018",
    "GO_Biological_Process_2018",
    "KEGG_2019_Human"
  )

DefaultAssay(combined) <- "integrated"
combined <- FindClusters(combined, resolution = 0.5)

```

Shown in the count table and UMAP plot, 16 cell clusters were predicted by Seurat package. 

```{r,echo=F,eval=F,message=T,warning=F,error=F}
table(combined$seurat_clusters)

Plot.cluster2D(combined, pt_size = 0.4, txt = "Predicted cluster")

DimPlot(combined, reduction = "umap", cols = as.vector(palette36.colors(36)[-2]),label = T)

```


```{r,echo=F,eval=T,message=F,warning=F,error=F}

p1 <-
  Plot.cluster2D(combined, pt_size = 0.4, txt = "Predicted cluster")
png(
  paste("Predicted_clusters.png", sep = ""),
  width = 3000,
  height = 2000,
  res = 300
)
p1
dev.off()
p1
```

# Cluster-specific genes


The Seurat package FindAllMarkers function was used to identify cluster-specific genes. For example, for cluster 0, the package performs differential testing using cells from cluster 0 vs all other cells. Only genes with log2 fold-change > 0.5 (logfc.threshold=0.5), and genes that are detected in a minimum fraction of 20% (min.pct=0.2) cells in either of the two populations were saved.

Considering too many tables or figures would drastically slow down the rendering of this report. I saved the Cluster-specific genes related results in the 'cluster_specific_genes' folder.


```{r,echo=F,eval=F,message=F,warning=F,error=F}
library(future)
plan("multiprocess", workers = 20)
plan()
getwd()
Idents(combined) <- combined$seurat_clusters
DefaultAssay(combined) <- "RNA"
# 30 mins
#cts_markers <- FindAllMarkers(combined, logfc.threshold = 0.5, min.pct = 0.2)
cts_markers %>%
  filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  write_csv("cluster_specific_genes_combined.csv")
i = 1
for (i in seq_along(levels(cts_markers$cluster))) {
  this_cluster <- levels(cts_markers$cluster)[i]
  cts_markers %>%
    filter(cluster == this_cluster & p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
    write_csv(paste0("marker_cluster_", this_cluster, ".csv"))
}


```


# Clusters UMAP plot split by condition

## Split by condition 

Number of cells for each cluster:
 
```{r,echo=F,eval=T,message=F,warning=F,error=F, fig.width=12,fig.height=8}
condition_idents <- as.factor(combined$orig.ident)
levels(condition_idents) <- c(rep("ctrl",6), rep("N1KO",6))
combined <- AddMetaData(combined, condition_idents, col.name = "condition")

table(combined$condition, combined$seurat_clusters)

```


```{r,echo=F,eval=T,message=F,warning=F,error=F, fig.width=12,fig.height=8}

Idents(combined) <- combined$seurat_clusters

p2 <-
  DimPlot(
    combined,
    reduction = "umap",
    split.by = "condition",
    cols = as.vector(palette36.colors(36)[-2]),
    ncol = 2,
    pt.size = 0.7
  )

png(
  paste("cluster_split_by_condition.png", sep = ""),
  width = 3000,
  height = 1500,
  res = 300
)

p2
dev.off()
p2

```

## Split by samples

Number of cells for each cluster:


```{r,echo=F,eval=T,message=F,warning=F,error=F, fig.width=12,fig.height=8}

table(combined$orig.ident, combined$seurat_clusters)

```


```{r,echo=F,eval=T,message=F,warning=F,error=F, fig.width=8,fig.height=16}

Idents(combined) <- combined$seurat_clusters

p3 <-
  DimPlot(
    combined,
    reduction = "umap",
    split.by = "orig.ident",
    cols = as.vector(palette36.colors(36)[-2]),
    ncol = 2,
    pt.size = 0.7
  )

png(
  paste("cluster_split_by_sample.png", sep = ""),
  width = 2000,
  height = 4000,
  res = 300
)

p3
dev.off()
p3

```

# Predict cell type using SCINA

SCINA and public marker gene database were used to annotate cell type automatically. 

This tool is a semi-automatic cell type annotation tool. It uses a marker gene list and gene expression values as input. It achieves good performance from this paper: A comparison of automatic cell identification methods for single-cell RNA sequencing data. 

- The input marker gene list is stored at 'provided_marker.csv'
- The gene expression plots of the cell types are stored in 'marker_plots' folder. 

Some of genes are not presented in the data, I have renamed some of these genes:

- T -> ?

- FLK1 -> KDR

- CD31 (PECAM-1) -> PECAM1

- SMA -> ?

- TE-7 -> ?


```{r,echo=F,eval=F,message=F,warning=F,error=F}

#combined <- FindClusters(combined, resolution = 0.1)
library(SCINA)
library(preprocessCore)
exp <- as.matrix(GetAssayData(object = combined[["RNA"]], slot = "data"))
# exp <- log1p(exp)
exp[] <- normalize.quantiles(exp)
rownames(exp) <- toupper(rownames(exp))

signatures <- preprocess.signatures("provided_marker.csv")
keep_scina_cell_type <- as.numeric(which(!sapply(signatures, function(x) {
  all(!x %in% rownames(exp))
})))
signatures <- signatures[keep_scina_cell_type]

results <- SCINA(exp, signatures,
  max_iter = 100, convergence_n = 20,
  convergence_rate = 0.99, sensitivity_cutoff = 0.9, rm_overlap = F, allow_unknown = T, log_file = "SCINA.log"
)
combined <- AddMetaData(combined, as.factor(results$cell_labels), col.name = "scina_cell_type")

Idents(combined) <- combined$seurat_clusters
annotate_cell_type <- as.factor(combined$seurat_clusters)
annotate_cell_type_name <- vector()
# iterate i as all seurat cell clusters index
for (i in 1:length(levels(Idents(combined)))) {
  this_cluster_cells <- WhichCells(combined, idents = levels(Idents(combined))[i])
  this_scina_obj <- combined[, this_cluster_cells]
  Idents(this_scina_obj) <- this_scina_obj$scina_cell_type
  # select the largest overlapped cell type name as annotation of this seurat cluster, the unknown is ignored
  if ("unknown" %in% levels(Idents(this_scina_obj))) {
    scina_obj_ident_length <- length(levels(Idents(this_scina_obj))) - 1
    max_ct <- names(which.max(table(Idents(this_scina_obj))[1:scina_obj_ident_length]))
  } else {
    scina_obj_ident_length <- length(levels(Idents(this_scina_obj)))
    max_ct <- names(which.max(table(Idents(this_scina_obj))[1:scina_obj_ident_length]))
  }
  annotate_cell_type_name <- c(annotate_cell_type_name, max_ct)
}


levels(annotate_cell_type) <- annotate_cell_type_name
combined <- AddMetaData(combined, as.factor(annotate_cell_type), col.name = 'scina_cell_type2')
Idents(combined) <- combined$scina_cell_type
p4 <- Plot.cluster2D(combined,reduction.method = "umap",pt_size = 0.5, txt = "SCINA cell type")
p4

png(
  paste("SCINA_raw_cell_type.png", sep = ""),
  width = 3000,
  height = 2000,
  res = 300
)
p4
dev.off()


Idents(combined) <- combined$scina_cell_type2
p5 <- Plot.cluster2D(combined,reduction.method = "umap",pt_size = 0.5, txt = "SCINA cell type")

png(
  paste("SCINA_cell_type.png", sep = ""),
  width = 3000,
  height = 2000,
  res = 300
)

p5
dev.off()
p5

# Save marker gene plots, only save used
# Note: created a tmp object for upper case rownames
#i <- 1
#total_scina_cell_type <- levels(as.factor(combined$scina_cell_type2))
#tmp_combined <- combined
#DefaultAssay(tmp_combined) <- "RNA"
#rownames(tmp_combined@assays$RNA@data) <- toupper(rownames(tmp_combined@assays$RNA@data))
#for (i in 1:length(total_scina_cell_type)) {
#  this_ct <- total_scina_cell_type[i]
#  this_markers <- signatures[[this_ct]]
#  for (j in this_markers) {
#    if (j %in% toupper(rownames(tmp_combined))) {
#      p_scina <- FeaturePlot(tmp_combined, j, ncol = 1)
#      png(
#        paste(
#          "SCINA_",
#          this_ct,
#          "_marker_",
#          j,
#          ".png",
#          sep = ""
#        ),
#        width = 1500,
#        height = 1500,
#        res = 300
#      )
#      print(p_scina)
#      dev.off()
#    }
#  }
#}
tmp_combined <- combined
DefaultAssay(tmp_combined) <- "RNA"
this_markers <- unlist(signatures)
for (i in seq_along(this_markers)) {
  p_scina <- FeaturePlot(tmp_combined, as.character(this_markers[i]), ncol = 1)
  png(
    paste(
      names(this_markers[i]),
      "_marker_",
      as.character(this_markers[i]),
      ".png",
      sep = ""
    ),
    width = 1500,
    height = 1500,
    res = 300
  )
  print(p_scina)
  dev.off()
}


```

In summary, the tools cannot accurately identify cell types. We might need annoate cell clusters manually.

```{r,echo=F,eval=T,message=F,warning=F,error=F}

Idents(combined) <- combined$scina_cell_type2
p1 <- Plot.cluster2D(combined,reduction.method = "umap",pt_size = 0.5, txt = "SCINA cell type")
p1

png(
  paste("SCINA_cell_type.png", sep = ""),
  width = 2500,
  height = 2000,
  res = 300
)
print(p1)
dev.off()
```

# Transfer cell types from Human Heart Atlas

- Here, we use the Human heart atlas as a reference dataset (486,134 cells, 11 major cardiac cell types, and 62 different cell states). The reference can be used to annotate cell types 

- [https://www.heartcellatlas.org/#DataSources](https://www.heartcellatlas.org/#DataSources)

![](https://www.heartcellatlas.org/assets/panel-1.png)

In summary, the transfered cell types are not separated well. We may need to adjust QC parameters and call cell lineage clones first. 


```{r,echo=F,eval=F,message=F,warning=F,error=F}
#library(SeuratDisk)

#Convert("heatglobal_raw.h5ad", "heatglobal_raw.h5seurat")

#heart_atlas <- LoadH5Seurat("heatglobal_raw.h5Seurat")

#qs::qsave(heart_atlas, "heart_atlas.qsave") 
```


```{r,echo=F,eval=F,message=F,warning=F,error=F}
heart_atlas <- qs::qread("heart_atlas.qsave")
options(future.globals.maxSize= 891289600)
heart_atlas <-
  FindVariableFeatures(heart_atlas,
                       selection.method = "vst",
                       nfeatures = 2000)

transfer.anchors <-
  FindTransferAnchors(
    reference = heart_atlas,
    query = combined,
    features = VariableFeatures(object = heart_atlas),
    reduction = "pcaproject",
    verbose = TRUE
  )
table(heart_atlas$cell_type)
  
celltype.predictions <-
  TransferData(
    anchorset = transfer.anchors,
    refdata = heart_atlas$cell_type,
    weight.reduction = combined[["pca"]],
    l2.norm = FALSE,
    dims = 1:30,
    k.weight = 30
  )

combined <- AddMetaData(combined, metadata = celltype.predictions)

```



```{r,echo=F,eval=T,message=F,warning=F,error=F}

Idents(combined) <- combined$predicted.id
p2 <- Plot.cluster2D(combined,reduction.method = "umap",pt_size = 0.5, txt = "Transfered cell type")
p2

png(
  paste("Transfered_cell_type.png", sep = ""),
  width = 2500,
  height = 2000,
  res = 300
)
print(p2)
dev.off()

```

```{r,echo=F,eval=F,message=F,warning=F,error=F}

```

```{r,echo=F,eval=F,message=FALSE,warning=F}
# Save Seurat object
# qs::qsave is much faster than save RDS

qs::qsave(combined, "combined.qsave") 
#combined <- qs::qread('combined.qsave')

```

```{r,echo=F,eval=F,message=F,warning=F,error=F}
# Manually save the rds, do not save on knitr
saveRDS(combined, "combined.rds")
```
